behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at real Git subprocess + parser handoff: rename detection comes from real `git` status/diff semantics (for example rename records vs add/delete pairs). A mock can hide format/exit/output edge cases. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely Git diff categorization with no AI/ML decision, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit baseline, perform a real rename, run the CLI, and assert report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`. This requires real filesystem + real `git` subprocess output.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears when CLI reads real Git binary diff output/metadata and handles non-text safely; mocked text fixtures can miss decode/stream behavior. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or multi-agent sequence in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file (for example random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is handling real Git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (real exit codes/stderr semantics). In-process mocks often flatten this and miss true subprocess behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior is Git/CLI error-handling only, with no AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real repo with no commits, run the CLI against it, assert zero-count/empty structured report and zero (or documented non-error) exit status.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history, which depends on true Git history traversal/diff semantics across commits. Mocking history output can hide real command behavior and corner cases. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML judgment or sequential agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Programmatically build a real repo history containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies across multiple commits, run CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from authoritative Git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at interaction between working tree/index state and real Git command selection; this is OS filesystem + Git index semantics, not just pure function logic. Mocked repo states can miss true staged/unstaged behavior. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic CLI/Git state handling, not AI/ML or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI in both states, and assert identical structured stdout output.
